product_overview: A lightweight single-page PDF reader that delivers an immersive, aesthetically pleasing reading experience in the browser while storing uploaded PDFs locally so users can browse, open, and resume reading without a backend.
core_goals: Let users upload and persistently store PDF files in the browser for later access
Provide a visually immersive dashboard that inspires and invites reading
Enable users to browse a local library of saved PDFs and open any book to read
Render PDFs in-browser with page navigation and resume-on-open behavior
Provide an in-document search experience (intercepting native find) to locate text inside an open PDF

features.name: Dashboard Experience
features.description: An immersive landing view that greets the user with visual flair, a rotating philosophical quote, subtle micro-animations, and actions to upload books or access the library.
user_flows: Navigate to / -> See immersive looping video background and randomized philosophical quote -> Open native file picker to upload a PDF -> Select a .pdf file -> See confirmation that the book was added to the library
Navigate to / -> See animated quote and micro-interactions -> Choose to open the Library view -> See the list of stored books
Navigate to / -> Open native file picker to upload a PDF -> Select a non-.pdf file -> See a validation message that only .pdf files are accepted and upload is prevented

features.name: Library Management
features.description: A persistent, client-side library that lists all uploaded PDFs saved in browser storage so users can discover and reopen books across sessions.
user_flows: Navigate to / -> Open Library view -> See list of saved books loaded from local browser storage -> Select a book to read -> See the Reader open with the selected book loaded
Navigate to / -> Open Library view -> See previously uploaded book present from last session -> Select that book -> See Reader open and land on the last-read page

features.name: Reader Interface
features.description: In-browser PDF rendering and reading controls that preserve the user's last-read position and provide an optimized in-document search experience.
user_flows: Navigate to / -> Open a book to read -> See PDF pages rendered in the reading interface -> Advance to the next page -> Return to a previous page -> See the current page position saved for future sessions
Navigate to / -> Open a book to read -> Press Ctrl+F to trigger the in-document search -> Enter a search term -> See matching results highlighted within the PDF
Navigate to / -> Open a book to read -> Press Ctrl+F to trigger the in-document search -> Enter a search term with no matches -> See a clear 'no results' message

routes.description: Main single page application managing views
